metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how dynamic per-repo project DET checks are integrated with the static `DET_CHECKS` tuple: either `run_det_floor(ctx)` grows a second phase that loads project checks from `ctx.repo_root` at call time (the PlanContext already carries `repo_root`), or a thin wrapper in `plan_review_precheck` calls project checks separately and merges results — the implementation path must be explicit so the repo-keyed loading is not accidentally collapsed into import-time.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify how dynamically loaded per-repo project DET checks are integrated with the currently-static `DET_CHECKS` module-level tuple in `det_floor.py`. `DET_CHECKS = (p1_readiness_shape, ..., p9_file_impact_coverage)` is a static constant; project invariant checks are data-driven from the overlay and must be resolved per `repo_root` at call time. The plan says the consumer runs 'appended into `run_det_floor`' but doesn't clarify whether `DET_CHECKS` becomes dynamically constructed or `run_det_floor` grows a second phase that loads project checks separately. A misimplementation that tries to insert callables into the static tuple at import time cannot be repo-keyed.
findings.location: ## Plan-review consumer integration / Integration point (clears G6 blocker 2)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the integration point section: 'Project DET checks are loaded from the overlay at call time using `ctx.repo_root`; `run_det_floor` appends them after P1–P9 in the same fail-open loop (an error in a project check → abstain, logged), OR a wrapper in `plan_review_precheck` calls `run_project_det_checks(ctx)` separately and concatenates `DetResult` lists before passing to `det_blocking_findings` / `det_advisory_findings` / `det_coverage`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a decision table (or pseudocode) to the plan specifying the `DetResult.blocking` assignment rule for all combinations of outcome×fail_mode×default_posture, so the consumer can be implemented deterministically (e.g. match+fail_mode:open → blocking=False; match+fail_mode:closed+default_posture:blocking → blocking=True; abstain+fail_mode:open → skip/pass; abstain+fail_mode:closed → blocking=True).
findings.finding: The `blocking` field assignment rule for a project-invariant `DetResult` is ambiguous: the plan says a `match` in a declared file 'blocks per the criterion's default_posture/blocking_enabled', and a `fail_mode:open` abstain is skip=PASS (blocking=False). But the `DetResult.blocking` field for the match-in-declared-file case is never given a decision rule that an implementer can mechanically apply — specifically whether `blocking=True` requires both `fail_mode:closed` AND `default_posture:blocking`, or just one, or is derived purely from `default_posture` on a match (irrespective of `fail_mode`).
findings.location: Plan remediation section 'Integration point': 'A match in a declared file blocks per the criterion's default_posture/blocking_enabled'; AC line: 'fail_mode gates abstain→block'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 2×2 table: rows = outcome(match|abstain), columns = fail_mode(open|closed); cells = (blocking bool, DetResult.status). Co-locate it with the DetResult shape description in the Integration point section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the exact JSON field name and matching semantics for the detector selector in the routing entry schema (e.g. `'detector': {'id': '...'}` for exact, `'detector': {'prefix': '...'}` for prefix), and add a concrete example DET invariant overlay entry to the plan or a schema comment.
findings.finding: The `detector` selector field added to the routing entry (described as 'id or id-prefix') has no schema definition: its exact key name, the matching semantics (exact equality vs prefix vs glob), and whether it is required or optional for `exec:DET` entries are never specified. An implementer writing `run_detectors` to read the routing map instead of the hardcoded `SECURITY_DETECTOR_PREFIX` must infer the field name and match logic.
findings.location: Plan section 'Data-driven map location (clears the hardcoded-prefix AC)' and AC line 1 ('no hardcoded rebar.builtin.security. prefix')
findings.suggested_fix: Add one concrete example routing entry in the plan, e.g.: `"project.no-banned-api": {"exec": "DET", "detector": "rebar.project.banned-api", "fail_mode": "open", "default_posture": "advisory"}`, and specify that `detector` is an exact-id match or a prefix terminated with `.`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify the relationship between the new `fail_mode` field and the existing `blocking_enabled` field — either that `fail_mode` supersedes `blocking_enabled` for DET entries (and the mapping), or that they coexist with a stated precedence rule, so `_validate_routing_entry` and both consumers can be implemented consistently.
findings.finding: The `fail_mode` field is introduced as a new routing-entry field, but the plan never reconciles it with the existing `blocking_enabled` field that already encodes equivalent semantics in `code_review/criteria_routing.json`. `apply_failclosed` currently reads `registry.threshold_for([crit])[1]` (which returns `blocking_enabled`). The plan says 'one code path parameterized by fail_mode, not two behaviors' but both `fail_mode` and `blocking_enabled` would exist in the schema after this story, with no stated rule for which takes precedence or whether `blocking_enabled` is deprecated.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section, third bullet ('Per-criterion fail_mode'); plan 'Data-driven map location' section; `src/rebar/llm/code_review/detectors.py` line 93

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC verifying that existing routing entries without `fail_mode` receive the documented default (open/closed per class), AND specify either an expand-contract migration sequence or a rollback plan for the `criteria_routing.json` schema change.
findings.finding: The plan describes `_validate_routing_entry` gaining `fail_mode ∈ {open, closed}` validation, but no AC verifies this validation is present or correct — and more critically, no migration strategy or rollback is specified for existing `criteria_routing.json` entries that currently lack the `fail_mode` field, which strict validation would break.
findings.location: Remediation section 'Data-driven map location'; Scope section; Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Apply the default in the loader (not the validator): if `fail_mode` is absent, apply the class-based default before validation, making the schema change backward-compatible without requiring a migration.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit tests for the fail_mode abstain paths: (a) fail_mode:open + engine-absent/parse-error/timeout → abstain resolves to PASS/skip, not BLOCK; (b) fail_mode:closed + engine-absent → abstain resolves to BLOCK (security class regression guard); these should be unit tests against the parameterized consumer logic, not just integration smoke.
findings.finding: The per-criterion `fail_mode` branching (open→abstain=PASS, closed→abstain=BLOCK) is gate-critical new logic, but no test is specified for the failure/abstain paths: engine-binary-absent, parse-error, and timeout under each `fail_mode` value. This is a new user-facing flow (gate outcome changes) with only happy-path coverage implied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Fail-open is preserved on engine-binary-absent / parse-error / timeout for fail_mode:open' + 'fail_mode gates abstain→block'
findings.suggested_fix: In the test plan, add three unit test cases for the `run_detectors` consumer: `test_failopen_engine_absent`, `test_failopen_parse_error`, `test_failclosed_engine_absent` — each asserting the correct `status`/`blocking` field in the returned `DetResult`. These are pure in-process and need no live binary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add or update at least one existing security-detector test to exercise the data-driven routing path (reading from `criteria_routing.json` built-in rows) end-to-end through the renamed `run_detectors`, asserting that a matched security pattern still produces a blocking `DetResult` with `fail_mode:closed`.
findings.finding: The refactor from hardcoded `SECURITY_DETECTOR_PREFIX` / `_criterion_for` to data-driven routing is a changed behavior for the existing security class, but no regression/modify-test work is specified to confirm that existing security-detector findings still route, block, and surface identically after the rename to `run_detectors`.
findings.location: 'Data-driven map location' section; AC: 'security class stays fail-closed (abstain→BLOCK) via the existing apply_failclosed'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the AC or scope section that the `exec=DET` check in `_descriptor_from_prompt` is a GUARD placed before the `prompts.get_prompt(...)` call (read `routing` first; if `exec=='DET'`, build the descriptor from routing fields without calling `get_prompt`).
findings.finding: The plan says the DET `_descriptor_from_prompt` branch 'skips `get_prompt`' but does not specify that this check must occur BEFORE the `prompts.get_prompt(...)` call in the function body. The current `_descriptor_from_prompt` calls `get_prompt` unconditionally at line 344 before reading `routing`. A DET criterion has no `plan-review-<cid>.md` prompt file; if the branch guard is placed after the prompt call (e.g. as a post-load fallback), `get_prompt` will raise `RegistryError` or similar before the branch executes.
findings.location: ## What — 'DET fallback descriptor shape (a `_descriptor_from_prompt` DET branch that skips `get_prompt`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC or Scope: 'The DET branch in `_descriptor_from_prompt` reads the routing entry first; if `routing.get("exec") == "DET"`, it returns the descriptor without calling `get_prompt` (the prompt call is in the `else` branch or after the guard).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete source field for `scenario` in the DET fallback descriptor (e.g. the detector registry rule's `message` field name, or the routing entry field that stores the human-readable description), and add a one-line example showing which record key it is read from.
findings.finding: The DET fallback descriptor's `scenario` field is described as 'detector rule message', but the source of that message — the detector record format returned by `engine_b.scan` — is not defined in the plan. The existing `detectors.py` reads `rec.get('detector_id')` and `rec.get('outcome')` and `(rec.get('location') or {}).get('file')`, but never a 'message' field. An implementer writing `_descriptor_from_prompt`'s DET branch cannot map 'detector rule message' to a concrete record field without reading the oracle internals.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section, third bullet: 'DET fallback descriptor shape ... scenario=detector rule message'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact consumer output when `file_impact` is absent and a repo match exists: either (a) emit a non-blocking `DetResult` with a defined status (e.g. `advisory`/`skip`) so coverage is tracked, or (b) suppress the result and emit a `coverage=N/A` signal — pick one and add it to the scoping AC or integration section.
findings.finding: When `file_impact` is absent, the plan says the DET invariant is 'advisory-only over the repo context' and 'never a hard block,' but does not specify whether the consumer emits a non-blocking `DetResult` (surfaced to the user as an advisory finding) or suppresses the result entirely (no DetResult emitted). An implementer must choose between these behaviors without guidance.
findings.location: Plan-review consumer integration → Match-scoping without a diff; Remediation AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the 'Match-scoping without a diff' subsection: 'When file_impact is absent, the consumer emits a DetResult with status=advisory (blocking=False) for each match, so the finding is visible but never gates the review; coverage is recorded as advisory-scoped.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend an existing one) that verifies `run_security_detectors` is aliased to the generalized `run_detectors` and that existing callers using the old name still pass.
findings.finding: The rename of `run_security_detectors` to `run_detectors` (with a thin back-compat alias) is described in the remediation body but no acceptance criterion verifies that the rename occurred or that the alias is present — this is uncovered deliverable work.
findings.location: Remediation section 'Data-driven map location'; Acceptance Criteria (original + remediation)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Merge the original AC2 and remediation AC2 into a single criterion that covers both the default-value assignment and the abstain→PASS/BLOCK branching, removing the duplicate.
findings.finding: Original AC2 and remediation AC2 are near-duplicates: both verify that `fail_mode` governs abstain→PASS vs. abstain→BLOCK behavior, differing only in phrasing. The original also folds in the default-value requirement, creating redundancy without additional coverage.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC2 ('Per-criterion fail_mode honored...'); Remediation Acceptance Criteria AC2 ('fail_mode gates abstain→block')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a persistent design-doc or CLAUDE.md update (or ADR) capturing: (1) the per-criterion fail_mode contract and its defaults, (2) the DET no-prompt descriptor shape and when to use it, and (3) the plan-review DET scoping rules (file_impact-scoped / advisory / N-A-fail-open) so follow-on stories have durable guidance.
findings.finding: The plan introduces multiple novel architectural decisions — a generalized data-driven scan consumer with per-criterion fail_mode, a DET fallback descriptor shape (no-prompt branch), and a new pipeline stage in plan_review — but names no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc update to capture them as persistent architectural artifacts.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `## Design notes` section to `CLAUDE.md` (or a new `docs/adr/det-invariants-generalization.md`) documenting: fail_mode semantics (open=skip, closed=block, security class default), the _descriptor_from_prompt DET branch contract, and the three plan-review scoping modes. Reference it from the routing schema's README/comment.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale in the Integration Point section for appending into `run_det_floor` vs. a separate call in `plan_review_precheck` (e.g. 'appending into `run_det_floor` ensures project DET results flow through the same fail-open exception wrapper and coverage recorder, without duplicating that logic in the caller').
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for integrating the project DET consumer by appending into `run_det_floor` (modifying the core floor function) versus calling a separate function in `plan_review_precheck` that returns `DetResult`-shaped results and is merged before the existing partition. The separate-function alternative avoids touching the static `DET_CHECKS` infrastructure, is independently testable, and keeps the core P1–P9 floor unchanged — a meaningfully lower blast radius for a data-driven, repo-keyed addition.
findings.location: ## Plan-review consumer integration / Integration point (clears G6 blocker 2)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit thin vertical-slice sequencing note: e.g., (1) land the data-driven generalization + fail_mode in code-review only with tests, (2) add the DET descriptor branch, (3) wire plan-review consumer — so each slice is independently merge-able and testable.
findings.finding: The plan touches ≥4 files (detectors.py, registry.py, routing schema, plan_review/ new consumer, det_floor.py implied) across ≥3 layers (schema/routing, code-review consumer, plan-review consumer, registry/descriptor, workflow_ops partition) with 7 AC items, but specifies no thin vertical-slice or evidence-gated MVP sequencing within the story — the implementation is a horizontal big-bang across all layers simultaneously.
findings.location: ## Scope / ## Acceptance Criteria / ## Plan-review consumer integration
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Sequencing' subsection: Step 1 — generalize detectors.py + routing schema + fail_mode (code-review only, all existing security tests still pass). Step 2 — add DET _descriptor_from_prompt branch in registry.py. Step 3 — wire plan-review consumer into det_floor + add file_impact scoping. Each step is a reviewable checkpoint.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
